changes.summary: When BCP mode is active, the attribute is no longer computed and must be set explicitly in configuration; when BCP mode is not active, it remains optional and computed from the API.
Now computed as well as optional so unused default fields no longer cause plan/apply inconsistencies when another mutually exclusive default is set.
Now computed as well as optional for string-list defaults; documentation clarifies mutual exclusivity with value and special.
Now computed as well as optional for special-value defaults; documentation clarifies mutual exclusivity with value and values.
Now computed as well as optional so unused default fields no longer cause plan/apply inconsistencies when another mutually exclusive default is set.
Now computed as well as optional for string-list defaults; documentation clarifies mutual exclusivity with value and special.
Now computed as well as optional for special-value defaults; documentation clarifies mutual exclusivity with value and values.
Read now preserves a null description from the API instead of coercing it to an empty string, avoiding null-to-empty-string plan inconsistencies.
Default create and update timeouts are now 120 minutes.
Failed creates roll back partially created decision tables even after a create timeout, and timeout errors suggest increasing the create timeout in a timeouts block.
When adding rows, a duplicate-row 409 at the expected row index is treated as a successful add instead of failing apply.
Read now preserves a null description from the API instead of coercing it to an empty string, avoiding null-to-empty-string plan inconsistencies.
Export now resolves customer_intent.id references to genesyscloud_intents_customerintents instead of genesyscloud_customer_intent.
Optional flag to enable article content search when creating the knowledge base (defaults to true); changing it after creation forces replacement.
Optional block to configure the IMAP server integration used to ingest inbound emails; populated from the API when present.
IMAP server integration ID to associate with the domain.
Computed IMAP server status from the API.
Optional block to configure the Graph API integration used for inbound and outbound email processing; populated from the API when present.
Graph API server integration ID to associate with the domain.
Computed Graph API server status from the API.
imap_settings and graph_api_settings are now read from the API and can be patched on create and update alongside existing mail-from and SMTP settings.
Optional block to configure a canned-response signature appended to outbound emails sent via the route.
Toggle to enable the signature on email send.
ID of the email signature canned response to use.
Whether the signature is included on every email or only the first email in a chain.
When to include the signature; valid values are Draft, Send, and SendOnce.
